I can confirm the numbers, but I would recommend a review.
Lucia, before we vote -- how certain are you that this framework will actually work?
What I can tell you with certainty is that it will reduce our exposure. I would not assert that if I could not defend it.
And the parts that are less clear?
Where the data is still thin, I have to hedge. This might create some exposure, so we should not defer the review.
So what exactly do you need from the board today?
My recommendation is simple. The board should approve the framework, and every team must follow it. Let me clarify one point first.
Please do.
This is not optional. Compliance is an obligation, and the early data is a clear indication that the risk is real.
That is clear. You have my vote.
What I can tell you with certainty is that our exposure is rising.
The board should prioritize this now.
Every team must follow the framework -- it is an obligation.
We should not defer this decision much longer.

Let me be precise about where we stand, because precision matters here. What I can tell you with certainty is this: our exposure is rising, and it is rising faster than last quarter. That is not a guess; the data is a clear indication. So here is my recommendation. The board should approve the new framework today, and every team must follow it. This is an obligation, not a preference. Now, I will be honest about what is less clear. The regulatory picture might shift in the next six months, so we may need a second review before the full rollout. But we should not defer the core decision. If we assert a clear standard now, we will control the risk. If we hedge on everything, we will control nothing.

Here is one habit that will change how you sound in any senior meeting. Before you open your mouth, decide how certain you really are, and then pick the modal that matches. If you are sure, say must or will, and say it plainly. If you are recommending, say should or ought to. And if you are honestly not sure, do not pretend. Use may or might to hedge, and people will respect you more, not less. The mistake I see most often is leaders who say will about everything. When every sentence is will, nothing sounds certain. Save your strongest modals for the things you can defend, and clarify the rest with might. Do that, and when you finally say we must, the whole room will lean in.

What I can tell you with certainty is that our exposure is rising. The board should prioritize this now. Every team must follow the new framework. This might create exposure at the regulatory level. We should not defer this decision much longer.
